rounds.domain: physics / condensed matter (Anderson localization in disordered lattices)
rounds.mechanism: Anderson localization: in a disordered quantum lattice, interference of multiply-scattered waves localizes the eigenstates within a localization length, producing a mobility edge separating extended from localized states; the localization length diverges at the mobility edge as a power law in disorder strength

Tuned mass damper (TMD): a secondary mass-spring-damper attached to a primary structure tuned to the structure's resonant frequency; the TMD absorbs vibration energy via destructive interference between primary and damper motion, broadening the effective damping spectrum

rounds.domain: earth systems / hydrogeology (karst aquifer conduit network)
rounds.mechanism: Karst aquifers route groundwater along preferential conduits dissolved in carbonate bedrock; flow is heterogeneous (turbulent conduits + slow matrix), strongly history-dependent (conduits enlarge with throughput), and gated at epikarst recharge boundaries; storm response is bimodal (fast conduit flow + slow matrix baseflow)

rounds.domain: ethology / behavioral biology (octopus chromatophore distributed control)
rounds.mechanism: Octopus skin patterning operates via millions of chromatophore organs, each a single muscle-cell-controlled pigment sac under local neuronal control. The pattern is computed bottom-up from local visual feedback at each skin patch with no central reference image; the global pattern emerges from distributed local matching where each motor neuron innervates a cluster of ~5-30 chromatophores

rounds.domain: law / common-law writs (habeas corpus / compulsory production)
rounds.mechanism: Habeas corpus is a writ compelling a custodian to produce a detained person before a court and justify the detention; the burden is procedural (production + justification) and the remedy is release if the justification fails. The structural element is a forced-production protocol with bounded discretion: the custodian cannot refuse to produce; the court cannot refuse to inquire; the standard for justification is statutory not discretionary

Thigmomorphogenesis: repeated mechanical stimulation (wind, touch) elicits a calcium-mediated mechanosensing cascade, ZFP/jasmonate signaling, and downstream growth-plan revision; the plant shortens stems, thickens diameters, and reinforces cell walls; the response scales with stimulus frequency × intensity

rounds.domain: mathematics / dynamical systems (Lyapunov exponent spectrum, Oseledets decomposition)
rounds.mechanism: The Lyapunov exponent spectrum quantifies, for a dynamical system, the exponential rates at which infinitesimally close trajectories separate along each direction of the tangent bundle; the Oseledets decomposition splits the tangent space into invariant subspaces each carrying one exponent; the signs of the spectrum classify the system (all negative: contracting; mixed: chaotic; positive: expanding)

rounds.domain: ethology / chemical communication (ant pheromone trail evaporation)
rounds.mechanism: Ant species deposit volatile alkanes/alcohols as pheromone trails that linearly decay by evaporation; reinforcement requires repeated trail-laying; if foragers stop reinforcing, the trail vanishes within minutes-to-hours depending on chemical volatility. The system has automatic obsolescence: stale information disappears without explicit deletion

Caldera eruption precursors include: (a) seismic swarms (changes in microquake rate and depth), (b) ground tilt + uplift, (c) CO2 / SO2 flux changes, (d) hydrothermal temperature anomalies. None is individually predictive; a multivariate, time-lagged combination is required. Machine-learning approaches identify ergodic precursor signatures that transfer across calderas

rounds.mechanism: Shear-thinning fluids have apparent viscosity that decreases with shear rate (e.g., Carreau, Cross models); below a critical yield stress they behave nearly rigidly, above it they flow with reducing resistance. The structural origin is shear-induced reorganization of microstructure (polymer disentanglement, particle alignment); the constitutive relation is non-linear in shear rate

rounds.domain: pedology / soil science (soil horizon emergence via differential weathering)
rounds.mechanism: Soil horizons (O, A, E, B, C, R) emerge by translocation, accumulation, leaching, and transformation operating at different depths over decades-to-millennia. The stratification is not pre-designed but emerges from differential weathering processes interacting; horizon boundaries are sharp despite the gradual nature of the process

rounds.domain: law / equity (bona fide purchaser for value without notice doctrine)
rounds.mechanism: The bona fide purchaser (BFP) doctrine: an innocent purchaser for value who takes property without notice (actual, constructive, or imputed) of prior adverse claims takes clean title, defeating prior equitable interests of which they did not have notice. The doctrine has a duty-of-inquiry requirement (BFP must make 'reasonable inspections') and protections against good-faith third-party transactions. Critically, the doctrine breaks the chain of prior tainted state

rounds.domain: earth systems / fluvial geomorphology (river meander cutoff and oxbow lake formation)
rounds.mechanism: A meandering river progressively widens its bends via differential erosion (outer bank) and deposition (inner bank); when bend curvature exceeds a critical threshold, the river breaches the narrow neck of land between adjacent bends and a 'cutoff' forms — the new straight channel takes over while the abandoned bend becomes an oxbow lake; cutoffs nonlocally accelerate upstream and downstream channel migration

rounds.domain: cartography (Töpfer's Radical Law for cartographic generalization)
rounds.mechanism: Töpfer's Radical Law (1961): when reducing map scale from S1 to S2, the number of features that should be retained is N2 = N1 × sqrt(S1/S2). The rule gives a principled count for selection at smaller scales; features that survive selection are those whose importance score exceeds a scale-dependent threshold. The law is empirical-cartographic, not information-theoretic, and incorporates feature category-specific exponents

rounds.domain: speleology (epigenic vs hypogenic speleogenesis; cave conduit network development)
rounds.mechanism: Speleogenesis produces conduit networks via either epigenic (descending acidic surface water) or hypogenic (rising deep-source) routes; the resulting cave geometry differs sharply (epigenic: branchwork following recharge patterns; hypogenic: maze-like with rising chimneys). Conduit growth is positive-feedback — flow enlarges conduit which enlarges flow — until thresholds destabilize the network

Thixotropic / yield-stress fluids exhibit time-dependent viscosity: apparent viscosity drops with sustained shear and recovers when shear stops; the recovery time and the yield-stress threshold are distinct parameters. Below yield stress the fluid is rigid; above, the time-dependent shear-thinning kicks in

Speleothems can record annual lamination via four mechanisms: fluorescent laminae (organic-matter flux variations), petrographic laminae (calcite texture or fabric variations), calcite-aragonite couplets, and trace element laminae. The annual signal is often imperfect (missed bands, false bands) due to hydrological irregularity

rounds.domain: chronobiology (zeitgeber entrainment and oscillator phase locking)
rounds.mechanism: An internal circadian oscillator entrains (phase-locks) to external zeitgebers (light, temperature, feeding); the entrainment depends on zeitgeber strength × phase response curve; multiple zeitgebers can act simultaneously and reinforce or conflict; phase-locking is bounded — outside an entrainment range the internal oscillator drifts free

rounds.domain: glaciology / glacial hydrology (englacial drainage via moulin-and-subglacial-conduit network)
rounds.mechanism: Surface meltwater forms supraglacial lakes that fracture vertically into moulins (10-100 m apertures); moulin water descends through englacial conduits to the subglacial bed where it forms Röthlisberger channels that route water to the glacier terminus on hours-to-days timescales; the network is bimodal (channelized vs distributed) and reorganizes seasonally as ice flow closes channels and pressure reopens them

Frequency seriation orders archaeological assemblages by stylistic similarity: each style follows a bell-curve popularity over time; assemblages with similar style frequencies are temporally close. The Brainerd-Robinson coefficient rearranges similarity matrices so that the diagonal contains the strongest similarities and similarities decrease monotonically away from it; correspondence analysis is the modern variant

rounds.domain: philology / textual criticism (stemmatic method for manuscript transmission)
rounds.mechanism: The stemmatic method reconstructs a manuscript family tree from extant copies via 'community of error implies community of origin': shared errors indicate common ancestry; the reconstruction posits hypothetical intermediates (hyparchetypes) and a root archetype; contamination (cross-borrowing) is flagged as a special case requiring stemma with dotted lines

rounds.domain: horticulture (scion-rootstock grafting and graft union formation)
rounds.mechanism: Successful plant grafting requires alignment of vascular cambium tissue between scion (above-graft) and rootstock (below-graft); a callus bridge forms, then new vascular cambium, then secondary xylem and phloem connect across the graft union. Compatibility scales with taxonomic proximity; incompatibility manifests as anatomical deformation at the junction. Specific signals across the graft union influence both scion and rootstock physiology

Coins are struck between two engraved dies (obverse and reverse); the die-axis (relative rotation between obverse and reverse) varies by 0-360 degrees. Ancient coins show 10% variation around standard orientation; modern coins are constrained to standard (180°) or medallic (0°). The die-axis distribution across many strikes from the same die-pair reveals striking-technique variance
